axis_responsibility: Brick: owns the declared work contract and required deliverables for this dashboard productization Brick.
Agent: design-lead returns observed design evidence and candidate contract text only.
Link: no Movement or route target is chosen in this return.
Support: dashboard server/UI/docs/export CLI/checkers are support mechanics and projection surfaces.
Evidence: baked dashboard-data.json is generated projection evidence and may be rebuilt from repo ledger evidence.
checker_or_verifier_plan: Add a dashboard productization kernel helper or fold into the existing read-side projection checker path; do not add a standalone check_*.py unless separately admitted.

Probe production fail-closed behavior by launching the Node server with NODE_ENV=production and no/dev INGEST_SECRET, POSTing to /ingest, and asserting non-2xx while GET static/dashboard-data remains available.
Static checker pins: server mentions NODE_ENV production guard, rejects dev-secret in production, and does not log/return secret values.
Hardcoded literal guard: scan support/dashboard and DEPLOY.md for Smith-specific text, concrete GCP project IDs, run.app URLs, and external Google font URLs unless explicitly parameterized/placeheld.

Bake round-trip: run the new bake verb, parse support/dashboard/public/dashboard-data.json, assert source_truth false and required dashboard packet keys, then verify server baked-snapshot loader can read it.
Movement UI guard: labels/page text must not present stop/return/pass as active Movement choices; forward/reroute remain Movement display labels.

Implementation proof commands expected later: npm --prefix support/dashboard run build; bake verb round-trip; production fail-closed probe; PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=support/import_identity python3 support/checkers/check_profile.py --all.
FIRE expectation: a mutant that tolerates dev-secret in production or removes the fail-closed branch must turn the production probe/checker RED.
returned.design_summary: Make support/dashboard third-party deployable by documenting delegated auth, keeping the app credential-free except env-only x-ingest-secret for POST /ingest, adding production fail-closed ingest behavior, adding an operator bake verb on the existing dashboard_export support module, neutralizing deployment-specific app text/URLs, and pinning the boundary in the existing read-side projection checker/profile lane. This is design support evidence only, not a source-truth or quality judgment.
edge_cases: Cloud Run + IAP can block programmatic ingest; reference deploy should rely on baked seed unless the operator deliberately exposes an authenticated ingest path.
Scale-to-zero clears in-memory participants; boot reloads baked snapshot and live POSTs remain projection updates only.
If a company does not protect GET routes at the reverse proxy/IAP/VPN layer, dashboard data is exposed; the app will not compensate with login.
Multiple participants and duplicate building IDs across projects require preserving participant_ref and composite project/building keys.
If INGEST_SECRET is rotated, producers using BRICK_DASHBOARD_INGEST_SECRET must rotate in the same operational window.
Removing external Google font/icon URLs requires either local bundled assets or UI fallback so icon names do not render as raw text.
invariants: Production /ingest must fail closed for missing INGEST_SECRET or INGEST_SECRET=dev-secret.
Static viewing may proceed without ingest enabled.
No deployment recipe may bake Smith org/project IDs, concrete GCP project IDs, run.app URLs, or credential bodies into app code/docs.
Reference Cloud Run + IAP is one recipe, not an app dependency.
Generic Docker and bare node recipes must work behind any external auth wall.
Generated support/dashboard/public/dashboard-data.json is projection output, not source truth.
Korean UI remains primary; no i18n framework is introduced.
not_proven: No implementation was performed in this read-only design return.
npm build was not run because this lane was read-only and build writes dist output.
Bake verb round-trip is not proven because the verb does not yet exist.
Production fail-closed runtime behavior is not proven in current code.
Full FIRE and check_profile --all outputs are not proven in this return.
Cloud Run + IAP recipe correctness is not proven against a live third-party deployment.
No Claude/Gemini model review was run in this return.
Provider quality, source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, and Movement authority are not proven.
observed_evidence: Read-only inspection only; no files edited. Worktree: /Users/smith/projects/BRICK-wt-dash on claude/track-dash.
Auth delegation judgment: the app should not grow login/session/account code. Current server exposes GET static/SSE/data and only gates POST /ingest with x-ingest-secret.
Current /ingest is not production fail-closed: INGEST_SECRET defaults to dev-secret and /ingest compares directly against that value.
Baked snapshot behavior exists: server loads DIST/dashboard-data.json at boot as default participant and GET /dashboard-data.json falls back to static file.
Existing export CLI can write arbitrary --out, but there is no dashboard-specific bake verb defaulting to support/dashboard/public/dashboard-data.json.
No support/dashboard/DEPLOY.md or dashboard README was observed.
UI has clear protocol wording leaks: Link page presents return/stop as Movement; labels.js maps Movement to forward/stop/return, while active Movement is forward/reroute only.
Hardcoded deployment-specific or non-neutral surface text exists: Cloud Run comments in server/Dockerfile, 브릭엔진 title, Smith comment in labels.js, and Google Fonts URLs in index.html.

Commands run read-only: node --check support/dashboard/server/index.mjs exited 0; PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=support/import_identity python3 -m brick_protocol.support.operator.dashboard_export --help exited 0.

proposed_changes: Add support/dashboard/DEPLOY.md with parameterized recipes: Cloud Run + IAP reference, generic Docker behind an authenticating reverse proxy with nginx snippet, and bare node. Document seed baking, INGEST_SECRET rotation, scale-to-zero semantics, and that source truth remains the repo ledger.
Change server ingest handling so NODE_ENV=production plus missing or dev-secret INGEST_SECRET disables /ingest while static viewing, /events, /healthz, and baked snapshot reads continue.

Extend support/operator/dashboard_export.py with a dashboard bake verb/function, preferably in the existing module, e.g. --bake-public writing support/dashboard/public/dashboard-data.json from dashboard_export_packet.
Neutralize adopter-facing app surfaces: replace 브릭엔진 title, remove Smith-specific source comments, make Cloud Run comments deployment-neutral, and remove or vendor external Google font/icon URLs.

Fix clear Link/Movement wording: display forward/reroute as Movement; display stop/hold/review/closure as lifecycle/frontier/state, not Movement. Adjust Building gate pass/stop labels if they imply judgment.

Add checker pins in the existing read-side projection profile and checker library: production ingest fail-closed probe/static branch pin, forbidden hardcoded deployment literals, bake packet shape/round-trip, and UI Movement literal guard.
relevant_current_structure: support/dashboard/server/index.mjs serves static dist, /events SSE, /dashboard-data.json fallback, and POST /ingest.
support/operator/dashboard_export.py builds dashboard_export_packet and dashboard_building_delta from existing ledger evidence and already has a generic --out CLI.
support/operator/report_sinks.py has explicit allow_real_dashboard_delivery and env-only dashboard ingest URL/secret handling.
support/checkers/profiles/read_side_projection_boundary.yaml is the existing profile home for dashboard/report/read-side boundary pins.
support/checkers/check_package_path_admission.py already admits support/dashboard as a scoped vendored dashboard surface and residue-filters support/dashboard/public/dashboard-data.json.
returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
unchanged_surfaces: No login, account, user table, cookie session, or app-owned auth system.
Authentication for GET dashboard viewing remains delegated to Cloud Run IAP, Cloudflare Access, VPN/Tailscale, or another authenticating reverse proxy.
Only app-level secret remains x-ingest-secret for POST /ingest, read from environment only.
SSE/event-delta model remains as-is; no scheduler, queue, retry, polling, database, or credential store.
No brick/, agent/, link/, or append-only project evidence mutation is part of this design.
Dashboard data remains a support projection, not source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, or Movement authority.
